title: Pavonia calcicola
taxonomy: Pavonia calcicola is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. The genus Pavonia is part of the order Malpighiales. Malpighiaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as barberry or acacia family. intro: Pavonia calcicola, also known as the rocky rosy mallow, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Pavonia calcicola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pavonia calcicola is commonly prepared as a salve or ointment to treat skin conditions, and also used in teas or infusions to promote overall health and well-being.
